title: Cronquistianthus glomeratus
taxonomy: Cronquistianthus glomeratus is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Heliantheae, which is a group of flowering plants. The species is further categorized under the genus Cronquistianthus, which is a genus of flowering plants native to the Americas. The Cronquistianthus glomeratus is a member of the order Asterales. The classification of Cronquistianthus glomeratus is an important part of its identification and understanding in the botanical community.

intro: Cronquistianthus glomeratus, also known as the Globe Groundsel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cronquistianthus glomeratus include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal agent.
